rationale: The citing paper gives Aribarg, Arora, and Bodur (2002) a specific role in its group decision-making literature review, explicitly describing that paper's focus on preference revision and concession using conjoint data. It then contrasts its own contribution against that approach, arguing for revealed-preference, observational structural modeling of buyer-user interactions in B2B organizational buying. This citation therefore functions as meaningful positioning of the paper's methodological and conceptual contribution, not just a passing background mention.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Aribarg et al. (2002) as a prior benchmark on modeling influence processes in group decisions, then positions its own work as moving from conjoint/stated settings to revealed-preference structural modeling in a B2B organizational context.
